explanations.1: The backslash at the end of line 1 tells the shell to continue this command on the next line, so lines 1-2 are actually one single command.
explanations.3: The 'cat << EOF' syntax is a way to write multiple lines of text into a file; everything between the opening EOF and closing EOF (line 6) becomes the content of reset.css.
explanations.27: toISOString() converts the current date and time into a standardized text format like '2024-01-15T10:30:45.123Z' that's easy to read in logs.
explanations.32: The 'async' keyword means this function can pause and wait for long operations (like downloading data) without freezing the rest of the code.
explanations.34: The 'await' keyword pauses here and waits for the fetch to finish downloading before moving to the next line.
explanations.39: 'DOMContentLoaded' is an event that fires when the browser has finished building the page structure, so it's safe to modify the page at this point.
